original: A Greenland shark is declared the longest-living vertebrate in the world at 392 years by an international team of scientists
The birth of hip-hop: DJ Kool Herc uses two turntables, playing the same record simultaneously to create the “merry-go-round” technique, at a party in the Bronx
Rudolf Povarnitsyn of the USSR sets a new high jump world record of 7'10¼" (2.40 m)
John Bray patents animation
Voyager 2 discovers two partial rings of Neptune
American astronomer Asaph Hall discovers Mars's moon Deimos
The US 4 x 100 m freestyle relay team ofMichael Phelps, Garrett Weber-Gale, Cullen Jones, and Jason Lezak beats France by 0.08 seconds to win gold and smash the world record at the Beijing Olympics

American super-swimmerMichael Phelpswins the men's 200 m individual medley in 1:54.66 at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics, becoming the third athlete and first swimmer to win four consecutive Olympic gold medals in one event
World's first roller rink opens in Newport, Rhode Island
Fictional girl group HUNTR/X from Netflix movie 'Kpop Demon Hunters' reaches number one on Billboard Hot 100, the first all-woman group to do so in 24 years (the last being Destiny's Child in 2001)
Viola Davis, American EGOT (Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, and Tony) and BAFTA Academy award-winning actress (The Help; Fences; Prisoners- Suicide Squad), born in St. Matthews, South Carolina
New York Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthbecomes the first MLB player to hit 500 home runs (off Willis Hudlin) in a 6-5 loss to the Indians at League Park, Cleveland
US premiere of The Beatles' debut film "A Hard Day's Night" in New York City
Jim Lee, Korean-American comic book artist, writer, editor, and publisher (X-Men; Superman: For Tomorrow), born in Seoul, South Korea[1][2][3]
Carl LewisduplicatesJesse Owens' 1936 feat, winning his fourth Olympic gold medal as part of the US 4 x 100 m relay team in a world record time of 37.83

Simone Manuel of the United States and Penny Oleksiak of Canada tie for the gold medal in the coveted 100 m freestyle at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics, matching the Olympic record of 52.70; Sarah Sjöström of Sweden takes bronze, her third medal of the Games
Andrian Nikolayev becomes the third Russian in space aboard Vostok 3
Total solar eclipse in India and northern France (2 minutes and 23 seconds)
Sicily records the highest-ever temperature in Europe at 48.8°C (119.8°F) in the city of Syracuse (unverified)
Explosive eruption blows 4,000 feet off Papandayan, Java, killing 3,000
"Monster" wildfire southeast of Bordeaux fought by 1,000 firefighters burns through 7,400 hectares (18,286 acres) during France's driest summer since 1961
Green Bay Packers football club is founded by George Calhoun andCurly Lambeauand is named after sponsor Indian Packing Company
Argentine soccer superstarLionel Messiconfirms signing a lucrative 2-year contract with French Ligue 1 champions Paris Saint-Germain after leaving FC Barcelona
Abhinav Bindra wins the men's 10 m air rifle at the Beijing Olympics, achieving the first individual Olympic gold medal for India and the country's first gold medal in any Olympic event since 1980

Japanese swimmer Kosuke Kitajima repeats the first leg of his Athens Olympics 100/200 m breaststroke double at the Beijing Games, winning the 100 m in a world record time of 58.91; he goes on to win the 200 m event
Fiji wins inaugural men's rugby sevens gold medal with a comfortable 43-7 rout of Great Britain at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics
Future four-weight world boxing champion Pernell Whitaker wins the lightweight gold medal at the Los Angeles Olympics
"Help!" a musical comedy film featuring the Beatles is released in the US
The Beatles arrive in Chicago, Illinois for their third (and final) US tour
First federal prisoners arrive at Alcatraz in San Francisco Bay
SOS is first used by an American ship, SS Arapahoe, off Cape Hatteras, NC
Harry S. Parmelee patents the sprinkler head
Harvey Hubbell patents an electric light bulb socket with a pull chain

The Fifteen Guinea Special is the last main-line steam passenger train service in Britain. A selection of British Rail steam locomotives makes a return 120-mile journey from Liverpool to Carlisle before having their fires dropped for the last time
"American Graffiti", directed byGeorge Lucas, opens in cinemas across the United States
Thousands of people evacuate near the Greek capital of Athens as large wildfires threaten the city after the hottest June and July on record
3114-08-11 BCE is traditionally considered the start date of the Mesoamerican Long Count calendar, used by several pre-Columbian Mesoamerican civilizations, notably the Maya
Milwaukee Braves future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherWarren Spahnrecords career victory number 300, beating the Cubs 2-1 at County Stadium, Milwaukee
Cincinnati Reds slugger Tony Perez becomes the first hitter to launch a home run into the red seats at Riverfront Stadium in Cincinnati, Ohio
$100,000 is raised in the US for thepedestal of the Statue of Liberty
Space Shuttle STS-43 (Atlantis 9) lands
Vostok 3 is launched, followed by Vostok 4 on August 12, 1962, marking the first time two spacecraft fly in orbit at the same time
Benin legalizes January 10 as a Voodoo holiday
British rock band Led Zeppelin performs for what becomes their final time in England at Knebworth Park, Hertfordshire, before over 200,000 fans
Beatles launch "Apple Records" label
Second studio album by Chic, "C'est Chic," is released, featuring the single "Le Freak" (Billboard Album of the Year 1979)
38 German aircraft are shot down over England
101,799 fans attend the soccer match between Brazil and France
Challenger flies to Kennedy Space Center via Davis-Monthan AFB, Arizona
Construction begins on the Louisiana Superdome
Satchel Paige, at 62 and needing 158 days on an MLB payroll to qualify for a pension, is signed by the Atlanta Braves, doesn't pitch, and becomes a coach
First double-century stand in Test cricket; Percy McDonnell (103) and Billy Murdoch (211) for Australia in drawn third Test vs. England in London
Phillies' Tug McGraw gives up a record fourth grand slam of the year
17th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: All-Stars 17, Philadelphia 7 (88,885 attendees)
PopeJohn Paul IIvisits Mexico
Chad declares Independence from France
Kingston Trio are the mystery guests on "What's My Line?"
First flight of the four-motor Cessna 620
US's first successful silver mill in Virginia City, Nevada
George B. Airy begins a 46-year tenure as England's Astronomer Royal
